commentary_text: The market assigns Turkey a 56% win probability versus the model's 36%, a 20 percentage-point gap favoring the underdog in the model. Both sources rank Turkey as favorite, but the market is substantially more confident. This divergence could reflect recent form, lineup availability, or injury status unknown to the model's Elo framework—information flowing into sportsbooks and traders in real time. The dashboard flags this as a signal worth monitoring: either the market has spotted something the model cannot see, or the model's structural estimate is holding against real-money pressure. Neither reading is certain from the numbers alone.
